technologies.name: Wazuh
technologies.evidence: A leading threat intelligence provider engaged Crest Data to build a native, production-ready integration with Wazuh, a widely adopted open-source SIEM and XDR platform.
technologies.reasoning: Wazuh is explicitly mentioned as the SIEM and XDR platform that Crest Data integrated with.

technologies.name: MITRE ATT&CK
technologies.evidence: MITRE ATT&CK technique mappings for suspicious files, and real-world exploit intelligence for detected CVEs.
technologies.reasoning: MITRE ATT&CK is explicitly mentioned as a framework used to provide tactic IDs, technique IDs, technique names, and behavioral signatures.

technologies.name: JSON
technologies.evidence: IOCs covering IPs, domains, URLs, and file hashes are stored locally in structured JSON files with enrichment metadata.
technologies.reasoning: JSON is explicitly mentioned as the data format used to store IOC data.

technologies.name: Cron Job
technologies.evidence: A cron job fetches the latest IOC threat lists from the threat intelligence platform across configurable threat categories.
technologies.reasoning: Cron Job is explicitly mentioned as the tool used to fetch IOC threat lists.
